title: oxalis ecuadorensis
intro: Oxalis ecuadorensis, also known as the Ecuadoran wood sorrel, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Oxalis genus. It is native to the Andean region and has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant is rich in ascorbic acid and anthocyanins, which are believed to possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, providing health benefits such as reducing oxidative stress and inflammation. Oxalis ecuadorensis can be prepared in various herbal forms, including infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various ailments, including digestive issues and skin conditions.
